go:39608.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: GA01
go:39608.question:
Out of the following four sentences, select the most suitable sentence with respect to grammar and usage.

  1. I will not leave the place until the minister does not meet me.
  2. I will not leave the place until the minister doesn't meet me.
  3. I will not leave the place until the minister meet me.
  4. I will not leave the place until the minister meets me.

go:39607.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: GA02
go:39607.question:
A rewording of something written or spoken is a __________.

  1. paraphrase
  2. paradox
  3. paradigm
  4. paraffin

go:39606.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: GA03
go:39606.question:
Archimedes said, "Give me a lever long enough and a fulcrum on which to place it, and I will move the world."

The sentence above is an example of a ____________ statement.

  1. figurative
  2. collateral
  3. literal
  4. figurine

go:39609.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: GA04
go:39609.question:
If 'relftaga' means carefree, 'otaga' means careful and 'fertaga' means careless, which of the following could mean 'aftercare'?

  1. zentaga
  2. tagafer.
  3. tagazen.
  4. relffer.

go:39610.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: GA05
go:39610.question:
A cube is built using $64$ cubic blocks of side one unit. After it is built, one cubic block is removed from every corner of the cube. The resulting surface area of the body (in square units) after the removal is ________.

  1. $56$
  2. $64$
  3. $72$
  4. $96$

go:39616.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: GA06
go:39616.question:
A shaving set company sells $4$ different types of razors- Elegance, Smooth, Soft and Executive.

Elegance sells at $\text{Rs.} \ 48$, Smooth at $\text{Rs.} \ 63$, Soft atÂ $\text{Rs.} \ 78$ and Executive atÂ $\text{Rs.} \ 173$ per piece. The table below shows the numbers of each razor sold in each quarter of a year.

$$\begin{array}{|c|r|r|r|r|} \hline \textbf{Quarter/Product} & \textbf{Elegance} &\textbf{Smooth} &\textbf{Soft} & \textbf{Executive}\\\hline \text{Q1} & \text{27300} &\text{20009} &\text{17602} &\text{9999}\\\hline \text{Q2} & \text{25222} &\text{19392} & \text{18445}& \text{8942}\\\hline \text{Q3} & \text{28976} &\text{22429} & \text{19544} & \text{10234}\\\hlineÂ \text{Q4} &\text{21012} & \text{18229} & \text{16595} & \text{10109}\\\hline \end{array}$$

Which product contributes the greatest fraction to the revenue of the company in that year?

  1. Elegance
  2. Executive
  3. Smooth
  4. Soft

go:39613.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: GA07
go:39613.question:
Indian currency notes show the denomination indicated in at least seventeen languages. If this is not an indication of the nation's diversity, nothing else is.

Which of the following can be logically inferred from the above sentences?

  1. India is a country of exactly seventeen languages.
  2. Linguistic pluralism is the only indicator of a nation's diversity.
  3. Indian currency notes have sufficient space for all the Indian languages.
  4. Linguistic pluralism is strong evidence of India's diversity.

go:39617.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: GA08
go:39617.question:
Consider the following statements relating to the level of poker play of four players $P,Q,R \ and Â \ S$.

  1. $P$ always beats $Q$
  2. $R$ always beats $S$
  3. $S$ loses to $P$ only sometimes.
  4. $R$ always loses to $Q$

Which of the following can be logically inferred from the above statements?

  1. $P$ is likely to beat all the three other players
  2. $S$ is the absolute worst player in the set
  1. (i). onlyÂ 
  2. (ii) only
  3. (i) and (ii) only'
  4. neither (i) nor (ii)

go:39611.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: GA09
go:39611.question:
If $f(x) =Â 2x^{7}+3x-5$, which of the following is a factor of $f(x)$?

  1. $\left(x^{3}+8\right)$
  2. $(x - 1)$
  3. $(2x - 5)$
  4. $(x + 1)$

go:39612.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: GA10
go:39612.question:
In a process, the number of cycles to failure decreases exponentially with an increase in load. At a load of $80$ units, it takes $100$ cycles for failure. When the load is halved, it takes $10000 \ \text{cycles}$ for failure.The load for which the failure will happen in $5000 \ \text{cycles}$ is _____________.

  1. $40.00$
  2. $46.02$
  3. $60.01$
  4. $92.02$

go:39663.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 1
go:39663.question:
Let $p, q, r, s$ represents the following propositions.

  • $p:x\in\left\{8, 9, 10, 11, 12\right\}$
  • $q:$ $x$ is a composite number.
  • $r:$ $x$ is a perfect square.
  • $s:$ $x$ is a prime number.


The integer $x\geq2$ which satisfies $\neg\left(\left(p\Rightarrow q\right) \wedge \left(\neg r \vee \neg s\right)\right)$ is ____________.

go:39636.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 2
go:39636.question:
LetÂ $a_n$ be the number of $n$-bit strings that do NOT contain two consecutive $1's$. Which one of the following is the recurrence relation forÂ $a_n$?

  1. $a_n = a_{n-1}+ 2a_{n-2}$
  2. $a_n = a_{n-1}+ a_{n-2}$
  3. $a_n = 2a_{n-1}+ a_{n-2}$
  4. $a_n = 2a_{n-1}+ 2a_{n-2}$

go:39661.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 04
go:39661.question: A probability density function on the interval $[a, 1]$ is given by $1/x^{2}$ and outside this interval the value of the function is zero. The value of $a$ is _________.

go:39634.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 05
go:39634.question: Two eigenvalues of a $3 \times 3$ real matrix $P$ are $(2+\sqrt {-1})$ and $3$. The determinant of $P$ is _______

go:39629.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 06
go:39629.question:
Consider the Boolean operator # with the following properties :

$x \# 0 = x, x \# 1=\overline{x}, x \# x = 0$ and $x \# \overline{x} = 1.$ Then $x\#y$ is equivalent toÂ 

  1. $x\overline{y}+\overline{x}y$
  2. $x\overline{y}+ \overline{x} \; \overline{y}$
  3. $\overline{x}y+xy$
  4. $xy+\overline{x} \; \overline{y}$

go:39649.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 07
go:39649.question: The $16\text{-bit}\;2's$ complement representation of an integer is $1111 \quad 1111 \quad 1111 \quad 0101;$ its decimal representation is ____________

go:39670.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 8
go:39670.question: We want to design a synchronous counter that counts the sequence $0-1-0-2-0-3$ and then repeats. The minimum number of $\text{J-K}$Â flip-flops required to implement this counter is _____________.

go:39632.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 09
go:39632.question: A processor can support a maximum memory of $4\;\textsf{GB}$, where the memory is word-addressable (a word consists of two bytes). The size of address bus of the processor is at least _________bits.

go:39667.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 10
go:39667.question:
A queue is implemented using an array such that ENQUEUE and DEQUEUE operations are performed efficiently. Which one of the following statements is CORRECT ($n$ refers to the number of items in the queue) ?

  1. Both operations can be performed in $O(1)$ time.
  2. At most one operation can be performed in $O(1)$ time but the worst case time for the operation will be $\Omega (n)$.
  3. The worst case time complexity for both operations will be $\Omega (n)$.
  4. Worst case time complexity for both operations will be $\Omega (\log n)$

go:39669.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 11
go:39669.question:
Consider the following directed graph:

The number of different topological orderings of the vertices of the graph is _____________.

go:39638.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 12
go:39638.question:
Consider the following "C" program.

void f(int, short);
void main
{
    int i = 100;
    short s = 12;
    short *p = &s;
    ____________;  // call to f
}

Â Which one of the following expressions , when placed in the blank above, will NOT result in a type checking error?

  1. $f(s, *s)$
  2. $i = f(i,s)$
  3. $f(i, *s)$
  4. $f(i, *p)$

go:39660.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 13
go:39660.question:
The worst case running times of Insertion sort , Merge sort and Quick sort, respectively are:

  1. $\Theta (n \log n)$, $\Theta (n \log n)$ and $\Theta(n^2)$
  2. $\Theta (n^2)$, $\Theta (n^2)$ and $\Theta(n \log n)$
  3. $\Theta (n^2)$, $\Theta (n \log n)$ and $\Theta (n \log n)$
  4. $\Theta (n^2)$, $\Theta (n \log n)$ and $\Theta (n^2)$

go:39673.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 14
go:39673.question:
Let $G$ be a weighted connected undirected graph with distinct positive edge weights. If every edge weight is increased by the same value, then which of the following statements is/are TRUE?

  • $P$: Minimum spanning tree of $G$ does not change.
  • $Q$: Shortest path between any pair of vertices does not change.
  1. $P$ only
  2. $Q$ only
  3. Neither $P$ nor $Q$
  4. Both $P$ and $Q$

go:39642.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 15
go:39642.question:
Consider the following C program.

# include <stdio.h>
void mystery (int *ptra, int *ptrb) {  
Â     int *temp; 
Â     temp = ptrb;  
Â     ptrb =ptra; 
Â     ptra = temp; 
} 
int main  { 
Â    int a = 2016, b=0, c= 4, d = 42; 
Â    mystery (&a, &b);
Â    if (a < c) 
Â          mystery (&c, &a); 
Â    mystery (&a, &d); 
Â    printf("%d\n", a); 
}

The output of the program is _________.

go:39640.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 16
go:39640.question:

$$S \rightarrow aS \mid bS \mid \varepsilon$$

  3. $\{a^n \mid n \geq 0 \} \cup \{b^n \mid n \geq 0\} \cup \{a^n b^n \mid n \geq 0\}$
  4. $\{ a,b\}^*$

go:39651.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 17
go:39651.question:
Which of the following decision problems are undecidable?

  1. Given NFAs $N_1$ and $N_2$ , is $L(N_1) \cap L(N_2) = \Phi$
  2. Given a CFG $G = (N,\Sigma,P,S)$ and a string Â $x \in \Sigma^{*}$, does Â $x \in L(G)$} ?

  4. Given a TM $M$, is $L(M)=\Phi$ ?
  1. I and IV only
  2. II and III onlyÂ

go:39647.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 18
go:39647.question:
Which one of the following regular expressions represents the language: the set of all binary strings having two consecutive $0$'s and two consecutive $1$'s?

  1. $(0+1 )^ *0011 (0+1)^* +(0+1)^*1100(0+1)^*$

go:39675.question:

x = u - t;
y = x * v; 
x = y + w;
y = t - z; 
y = x * y;

The minimum number of total variables required to convert the above code segment to static single assignment form is __________.

go:39655.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 20
go:39655.question:
Consider an arbitrary set of CPU-bound processes with unequal CPU burst lengths submitted at the same time to a computer system. Which one of the following process scheduling algorithms would minimize the average waiting time in the ready queue?

  1. Shortest remaining time first
  2. Round-robin with the time quantum less than the shortest CPU burstÂ 
  3. Uniform random
  4. Highest priority first with priority proportional to CPU burst length

  1. $VXYZ$
  2. $VWXZ$
  3. $VWXY$
  4. $VWXYZ$

go:39644.question:

  1. Atomicity
  2. Consistency
  3. Isolation
  4. Deadlock-freedom

go:39646.question:
A database of research articles in a journal uses the following schema.

$\text{(VOLUME, NUMBER, STARTPAGE, ENDPAGE, TITLE, YEAR, PRICE)}$

The primary key is '$\text{(VOLUME, NUMBER, STARTPAGE, ENDPAGE)}$

and the following functional dependencies exist in the schema.

$\begin{align*}&(\text{VOLUME , NUMBER, STARTPAGE, ENDPAGE}) & \rightarrow \text{TITLE} \\ &(\text{VOLUME, NUMBER}) & \rightarrow Â \text{ YEAR} \\ &(\text{VOLUME, NUMBER, STARTPAGE, ENDPAGE}) Â  Â  Â  Â &\rightarrow Â \text{PRICE}Â \end{align*}$

The database is redesigned to use the following schemas

$(\text{VOLUME, NUMBER, STARTPAGE, ENDPAGE, TITLE, PRICE}) \\ \text{(VOLUME, NUMBER, YEAR})$

Which is the weakest normal form that the new database satisfies, but the old one does not?

  1. $\text{1NF}$
  2. $\text{2NF}$
  3. $\text{3NF}$
  4. $\text{BCNF}$

  1. $\textsf{DNS}$
  2. $\textsf{ARP}$

go:39717.question:
A function $f: \Bbb{N^+} \rightarrow \Bbb{N^+}$ , defined on the set of positive integers $\Bbb{N^+}$, satisfies the following properties:

Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â $f(n)=f(n/2)$ Â Â if $n$ is even

Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â Â $f(n)=f(n+5)$ Â if $n$ is odd

go:39709.question:
Consider the following experiment.

Step 1. Flip a fair coin twice.

Step 2. If the outcomes are (TAILS, HEADS) then output $Y$ and stop.

Step 3. If the outcomes are either (HEADS, HEADS) or (HEADS, TAILS), then output $N$ and stop.

Step 4. If the outcomes are (TAILS, TAILS), then go to Step $1.$

The probability that the output of the experiment is $Y$ is (up to two decimal places)

go:39698.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 31
go:39698.question: The size of the data count register of a $\text{DMA}$ controller is $16\;\text{bits}$. The processor needs to transfer a file of $29,154$ kilobytes from disk to main memory. The memory is byte addressable. go:39691.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 32
go:39691.question: The stage delays in a $4$-stage pipeline are $800, 500, 400$ and $300$ picoseconds. go:39688.question:

  1. $\Theta (1)$
  2. $\Theta (\log(n))$
  3. $\Theta (\sqrt{n})$

go:39704.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 34
go:39704.question:

go:39706.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 37
go:39706.question:
An operator $delete(i)$ for a binary heap data structure is to be designed to delete the item in the $i$-thÂ  node. Assume that the heap is implemented in an array and $i$ refers to the $i$-th index of the array. If the heap tree has depth $d$ (number of edges on the path from the root to the farthest leaf ), then what is the time complexity to re-fix the heap efficiently after the removal of the element?

  1. Â $O(1)$Â 
  2. Â $O(d)$ but not $O(1)$Â 
  3. Â $O(2^d)$Â but not $O(d)$Â

go:39731.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 38
go:39731.question:
Consider the weighted undirected graph with $4$ vertices, where the weight of edge $\{i,j\}$ is given by the entry $W_{ij}$ in the matrix $W$.

Â W=$\begin{bmatrix} 0&2 &8 &5 \\ 2&0 &5 &8 \\ 8&5 &0 &x \\ 5& 8 &x &0 \end{bmatrix}$

go:39725.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 39
go:39725.question: Let $G$ be a complete undirected graph on $4$ vertices, having $6$ edges with weights being $1, 2, 3, Â 4, 5,$ and $6$. The maximum possible weight that a minimum weight spanning tree of $G$ can have is __________

go:39727.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 40
go:39727.question:

  1. I only.
  2. II only.
  3. Both I and II.
  4. Neither I nor II.

go:39684.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 41
go:39684.question:
Let $Q$ denote a queue containing sixteen numbers and $S$ be an empty stack. $Head(Q)$ returns the element at the head of the queue $Q$ without removing it from $Q$. Similarly $Top(S)$ returns the element at the top of $S$ without removing it from $S$. Consider the algorithm given below.

while Q is not Empty do 
  if S is Empty OR Top(S) â‰¤ Head (Q) then 
Â     x:= Dequeue (Q); 
Â     Push (S, x); 
  else  
Â     x:= Pop(S); 
Â     Enqueue (Q, x);
  end
 end

The maximum possible number of iterations of the while loop in the algorithm is _______.

go:39705.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 42
go:39705.question:

$G_1 : S \to aS \mid B, B \to b \mid bB$

  4. Â $\{ a^mb^n \mid m \geq 0 \text{ and } n >0\}$ and $\{ a^mb^n \mid m > 0 \text{ or } n>0\}$

go:39732.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 43
go:39732.question:

go:39721.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 44
go:39721.question:
Let $X$ be a recursive language and $Y$ be a recursively enumerable but not recursive language. Let $W$ Â and $Z$ be two languages such that $\overline{Y}$ reduces to $W$, and $Z$ reduces to $\overline{X}$ (reduction means the standard many-one reduction). Which one of the following statements is TRUE?

  3. $W$ is not recursively enumerable and $Z$ is recursive.

go:39697.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 45
go:39697.question:
The attribute of three arithmetic operators in some programming language are given below.
$$\begin{array}{|c|l|}\hline \textbf{OPERATOR} Â & Â \textbf{PRECEDENCE} & \textbf{ASSOCIATIVITY} & \textbf{ARITY} \\\hline Â \text{$+$} & \text{High} & \text{Left} & \text{Binary} \\\hline Â \text{$-$} & \text{Medium} & \text{Right} & \text{Binary} \\\hline Â \text{$*$} & \text{Low} & \text{Left} & \text{Binary} \\\hline \end{array}$$
The value of the expression $2-5+1-7*3$ in this language is ________.

go:39700.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 46
go:39700.question:
Consider the following Syntax Directed Translation Scheme $( SDTS )$, with non-terminals $\{S,A \}$ and terminals $\{a,b \}$.

Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  $S \to aA Â \quad \{\text{print }1\}$

Â  Â  Â  Â  Â Â $S \to a Â  Â \quad Â  \{\text{print }2\}$

Â Â  Â  Â  Â  Â $A \to Sb Â \quad \{\text{print }3\}$

Using the above $SDTS$ , the output printed by a bottom-up parser, for the input $aab$ is:

  1. Â $1 \ 3 \ 2 $Â 
  2. Â $2 \ 2 \ 3 Â $Â 
  3. Â $2 \ 3 \ 1 $Â 
  4. Â syntax error

go:39690.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 47
go:39690.question: Consider a computer system with $40$-bit virtual addressing and page size of sixteen kilobytes. If the computer system has a one-level page table per process and each page table entry requires $48$ bits, then the size of the per-process page table is __________ megabytes.

go:39716.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 48
go:39716.question: Cylinder a disk queue with requests for $I/O$ to blocks on cylinders $47, 38, 121, 191, 87, 11, 92, 10.$ The C-LOOK scheduling algorithm is used. The head is initially at cylinder number $63$, moving towards larger cylinder numbers on its servicing pass. The cylinders are numbered from $0$ to $199$. The total head movement (in number of cylinders) incurred while servicing these requests is__________.

go:39711.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 49
go:39711.question: Consider a computer system with ten physical page frames. The system is provided with an access sequence $(a_{1}, a_{2},....,a_{20}, a_{1}, a_{2},...a_{20})$, where each $a_{i}$ is a distinct virtual page number. The difference in the number of page faults between the last-in-first-out page replacement policy and the optimal page replacement policy is_________.

go:39719.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 50
go:39719.question:
Consider the following proposed solution for the critical section problem. There are $n$ processes : $P_0....P_{n-1}$. In the code, function $\text{pmax}$ returns an integer not smaller than any of its arguments .For all $i,t[i]$ is initialized to zero.

Code for $P_i$;

do {
    c[i]=1; t[i]= pmax (t[0],....,t[n-1])+1; c[i]=0;
    for every j != i in {0,....,n-1} {
        while (c[j]); 
        while (t[j] != 0 && t[j] <=t[i]);
    }
    Critical Section;
    t[i]=0;
    
    Remainder Section;
    
}  while (true);

Which of the following is TRUE about the above solution?

  1. At most one process can be in the critical section at any time
  2. The bounded wait condition is satisfied
  3. The progress condition is satisfied
  4. It cannot cause a deadlock

go:39703.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 51
go:39703.question:
Consider the following two phase locking protocol. Suppose a transaction $T$ accesses (for read or write operations), a certain set of objects $\{O_1,\ldots,O_k \}$. This is done in the following manner:

  • $\text{Step 1}$. $T$ acquires exclusive locks to $O_1,\ldots,O_k$ in increasing order of their addresses.
  • $\text{Step 2}$. The required operations are performed .
  • $\text{Step 3}$. All locks are released

This protocol will

  1. Â guarantee serializability and Â deadlock-freedomÂ 
  2. Â guarantee neither serializability nor deadlock-freedomÂ 
  3. Â guarantee serializability but not deadlock-freedom Â 
  4. Â guarantee deadlock-freedom but not serializability.

go:39694.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 52
go:39694.question:
Consider that $B$ wants to send a message $m$ that is digitally signed to $A$. Let the pair of private and public keys for $A$ and $B$ be denoted by ${K_{x}}^-$ and ${K_{x}}^+$ for $x=A, B$, respectively. Let $K_{x}(m)$ represent the operation of encrypting $m$ with a key $K_{x}$ and $H(m)$ represent the message digest. Which one of the following indicates the CORRECT way of sending the message $m$ along with the digital signature to $A$?

  1. $\left\{m, {K_{B}}^+(H(m))\right\}$
  2. $\left\{m, {K_{B}}^-(H(m))\right\}$
  3. $\left\{m, {K_{A}}^-(H(m))\right\}$
  4. $\left\{m, {K_{A}}^+(m)\right\}$

go:39712.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 53
go:39712.question:
An IP datagram of size $1000$ $\text{bytes }$arrives at a router. The router has to forward this packet on a link whose MTU (maximum transmission unit) is $100$ $\text{bytes }$. Assume that the size of the IP header is $20$ $\text{bytes }.$

The number of fragments that the IP datagram will be divided into for transmission is________.

go:39720.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 54
go:39720.question: For a host machine that uses the token bucket algorithm for congestion control, the token bucket has a capacity of $1$ $\text{megabyte}$ and the maximum output rate is $20$ $\text{megabytes}$ per $\text{second}$. Tokens arrive at a rate to sustain output at a rate of $10$ $\text{megabytes}$ per $\text{second}$. The token bucket is currently full and the machine needs to send $12$ $\text{megabytes}$ of data. The minimum time required to transmit the data is _____________ $\text{seconds}$.

go:39696.title: GATE CSE 2016 Set 1 | Question: 55
go:39696.question:
A sender uses the Stop-and-Wait $\text{ARQ}$ protocol for reliable transmission of frames. Frames are of size $1000$ bytes and the transmission rate at the sender is $80\;\textsf{Kbps} (1\textsf{Kbps} = 1000\;\text{ bits/second}).$ Â Size of an acknowledgment is $100$ bytes and the transmission rate at the receiver is $8\;\textsf{Kbps.}$ The one-way propagation delay is $100$ milliseconds.

Assuming no frame is lost, the sender throughput is ________ bytes/ second.
